run_summary: Screen 1 is holding 6 positions (9-7 days old, mixed P&L) while 16 new flags from two distinct AI catalysts (Google Gemini/Android + Meta AI Mode) hit the desk. Most flags are clean sympathy fades, but several show signs of genuine underlying pressure or misalignment with the trigger. Basket rule applies to both triggers.

entries.name: Verra Mobility Corporation
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Google Home consumer speaker has zero overlap with municipal photo-enforcement contracts, court-admissible chain of custody, or the physical camera infrastructure VRRM operates. The sharp drop on light volume looks like indiscriminate AI-basket selling.
entries.what_kills: News of a municipality piloting AI-only enforcement (no physical cameras); a key Commercial Services customer non-renewal (the 10-Q flagged one significant CS customer in negotiation); Ontario-style legislative bans spreading.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings evidence is clean: municipal photo enforcement under multi-year NYCDOT contract (17.9% revenue) is structurally unaffected by Google Home speaker release. Move is -6.18% on 0.52x volume — light volume sympathy classic. Five-day change +0.64% shows minimal downside momentum. This is a Gemini-trigger basket (see MANH, BLKB, OSPN, TYL, OMCL, PLAB, ONTO, AEIS, DOCN below). Basket rule applies: sizing all Google Gemini/Android flags together as a single thesis test, defaulting to exploratory tier.

red_team.weakest_link: The 'what_kills' criterion about a municipality piloting AI-only enforcement is speculative; no current evidence suggests municipal customers are evaluating AI-native alternatives to physical camera enforcement.
red_team.critique: The bull case is mechanically sound: Google's Gemini smart speaker has no overlap with municipal photo-enforcement contracts, regulatory compliance chains, or physical camera hardware. The five-day change of +0.64% shows no sustained downside momentum, and the -6.18% single-day move on 0.52x volume is consistent with light-volume sympathy selling. No specific news about NYCDOT contract execution delays, customer attrition, or competitive threat from AI-native enforcement systems was cited.

entries.name: Blackbaud, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't replace nonprofit fundraising / donor management workflows with embedded payment processing and 20+ years of historical donor data. Blackbaud's own filings explicitly warn that AI sentiment can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals — this is that pattern.
entries.what_kills: A nonprofit-focused AI startup gaining material traction with enterprise nonprofits; renewal-rate compression in upcoming quarter; new Security Incident developments.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Nonprofit donor-management with 20+ years of embedded customer data, payment processing, and tax-exempt workflows cannot be replaced by Gemini smart speaker. Filings explicitly flag that AI sentiment can drive stock volatility independent of fundamentals — this is that pattern. Move -4.43% on 1.01x volume, five-day -5.54%. Part of Gemini basket — exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case relies on the explicit Risk Factor disclosure about AI sentiment driving volatility, but filings disclaiming a risk do not insulate a stock from repricing if the broader market remains unconvinced.
red_team.critique: The five-day decline of -5.54% and single-day move of -4.43% on near-normal volume (1.01x) show moderate but not extended downside. The thesis that a Gemini smart speaker cannot replace nonprofit fundraising workflows with two decades of embedded donor data is mechanically sound. No concrete news about nonprofit customer migration to AI tools, renewal-rate compression, or new security incidents was cited as a driver of the decline.

entries.name: OneSpan Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker doesn't authenticate banking customers or sign legal documents. OSPN's regulated authentication and e-signature business is bound to PSD2/eIDAS standards. The move is small but on lighter volume, classic indiscriminate basket selling.
entries.what_kills: A major bank publicly choosing an AI-native authentication vendor over OSPN; FIDO Alliance changes that disadvantage OSPN.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Regulated FIDO2/passkey authentication and e-signature compliance are standards-bound, not vulnerable to a consumer OS feature release. Move -3.72%, light volume 0.87x, five-day -2.83%. Part of Gemini basket — exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The bull case assumes FIDO2 standards-bound authentication is safe from disruption, but if a hyperscaler deploys a competing authentication standard endorsed by major banks, OSPN's regulatory moat could weaken.
red_team.critique: The move is modest (-3.72%) on light volume (0.87x), and the five-day change of -2.83% shows minimal downside momentum. The mechanical claim that a consumer OS feature release cannot affect regulated authentication standards is sound. No news about FIDO Alliance changes, major bank authentication vendor switches, or competitive encroachment was cited.

entries.name: Tyler Technologies, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: No government CIO is replacing court records of record with a Google Home speaker. TYL's moat is FedRAMP/state-specific compliance and entrenched multi-decade municipal relationships. The move on very light volume (0.65x) is classic indiscriminate AI fade.
entries.what_kills: A government-specific AI-native competitor winning a flagship state contract; FedRAMP changes that advantage AI-native vendors.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Government court records and municipal procurement are FedRAMP/compliance-bound with year-long sales cycles and multi-decade contracts. Gemini smart speaker has zero mechanical link. Move -3.54% on very light volume 0.65x. Part of Gemini basket — exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The very light volume of 0.65x limits the credibility of the signal; a thin-volume move on a Google AI announcement is easily reversed and offers weak evidence of systematic repricing.
red_team.critique: The -3.54% single-day move on 0.65x volume is classic noise on low participation. The five-day change of -4.99% is moderate but not extended. The thesis that government court-records systems are insulated from Gemini releases by FedRAMP compliance, year-long sales cycles, and decades of entrenched contracts is well-founded. No news about government-specific AI competitors, FedRAMP changes, or bookings deterioration was cited.

entries.name: Onto Innovation Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: Process-control metrology for advanced nodes is a direct beneficiary of AI-chip manufacturing complexity — more compute = smaller geometries = more inspection passes per wafer. A Gemini-on-Android release has no mechanical link to wafer fab inspection demand. The sharp drop on light volume looks like ETF-driven semi-cap sympathy with an already-extended five-day base.
entries.what_kills: Memory market downturn affecting Onto's specialty-semi exposure; delay or unfavorable terms on the Rigaku transaction; broader semi-cap capex air pocket.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Process-control metrology for advanced nodes is a direct beneficiary of AI-chip manufacturing complexity. Filings show AI as operational risk only, not competitive threat. Move -5.9% on light volume 0.75x, but five-day change +15.37% is strongly positive — this is an overshoot on technical momentum. Part of Gemini/Android basket — exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day change of +15.37% is strongly positive, suggesting the stock has already repriced upward on technical/semiconductor momentum; the -5.9% single-day drop may be a normal pullback on that run rather than a durable repricing from the Gemini announcement.
red_team.critique: The bull case correctly identifies that advanced-node chip manufacturing drives demand for process-control metrology, and Gemini/Android releases are mechanically unrelated to wafer fab inspection. The five-day momentum of +15.37% contradicts any narrative of sustained selling pressure; the single-day -5.9% on 0.75x volume appears to be a pullback on an already-extended rally, not a new selloff. No news about memory-market downturns, Rigaku deal delays, or semi-capex reductions was cited.

entries.name: Advanced Energy Industries, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: AEIS's own 10-K identifies AI infrastructure as a material growth driver. Power conversion for fabs and AI data centers is structurally long the buildout that Gemini, ChatGPT, and Claude inference all require. A consumer OS release has zero mechanical impact on data-center power-delivery demand. The sympathy drop on weak volume with a still-positive five-day looks like a clean overshoot.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler capex digestion narrative gaining traction; loss of design-win share to a competitor; Data Center Computing margins continuing to compress as that segment grows.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='none', panic_calibration='unjustified'. 10-K explicitly identifies AI infrastructure (data-center power delivery) as stated revenue tailwind. Move -5.45% on light volume 0.67x, but five-day +12.45% shows strong underlying momentum being trampled by sympathy. Part of Gemini/Android basket — exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The 10-K disclosure that AI infrastructure is a growth driver does not guarantee that the data-center power-delivery segment will perform as expected; execution risk and competitive pricing pressure remain material.
red_team.critique: The move is -5.45% on light volume (0.67x), but the five-day change of +12.45% is strongly positive and contradicts the sympathy-selling thesis. The bull case correctly observes that AEIS's own filings identify AI data-center infrastructure as a demand tailwind, making a Gemini announcement mechanically backwards as a bearish catalyst. No news about hyperscaler capex digestion, design-win losses, or margin compression in the Data Center Computing segment was cited.

entries.name: DigitalOcean Holdings, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: DOCN's SMB/indie-developer cloud is orthogonal to a consumer Android+Gemini release. Their AI inference offerings (GPU droplets, GenAI platform) actually align them with the trend. The mechanical link from 'Google ships Gemini in Android' to 'DigitalOcean's SMB cloud subscriptions decline' is nonexistent.
entries.what_kills: Hyperscaler price-cuts on small instances making DOCN's pricing edge less meaningful; AI/ML revenue traction failing to materialize in next quarter; SMB churn acceleration.
entries.reasoning: Auto-converted: exploratory tier cap reached (6 simultaneous open). Original reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. 10-K frames AI/ML as growth opportunity, not threat. SMB cloud orthogonal to consumer Android feature. Move -3.92% on light volume 0.52x, five-day +3.48% shows no downside momentum. Confidence at threshold but meets BUY eligibility (confidence 3 = min_buy_confidence). Part of Gemini basket — exploratory tier per basket rule.

red_team.weakest_link: The confidence level in the bull decision is only 3 (at the minimum buyable threshold), and the SMB cloud narrative is less defensible than the infrastructure/government/vertical-SaaS theses in the rest of the basket.
red_team.critique: The move is -3.92% on very light volume (0.52x), and the five-day change of +3.48% shows no downside momentum at all. The thesis that an SMB cloud platform is orthogonal to a consumer Android feature is sound, and DOCN's AI inference offerings actually align with the Gemini trend. The confidence level of 3 indicates marginal conviction, but no specific news about hyperscaler price-cutting, failure of AI/ML revenue traction, or SMB churn acceleration was cited to contradict the bull case.

entries.name: Manhattan Associates, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: Google Home speakers don't compete with enterprise warehouse management systems. MANH's moat is multi-year deployments integrating with physical warehouse hardware and ERP systems — switching cost is the moat. Already extended over five days, suggesting broad SaaS de-rating rather than name-specific impairment.
entries.what_kills: A hyperscaler announcing an AI-native WMS replacement; a Tier-1 retailer publicly migrating off MANH to an LLM-orchestrated supply chain platform; further extension of the 5-day decline on rising volume.
entries.reasoning: Killed by red-team. Weakest link: The five-day decline of -9.0% combined with the -5.24% single-day move suggests the selloff has already materialized and may reflect broader SaaS sector concerns beyond AI-event sympathy. Bear case: While the bull thesis that enterprise WMS systems have deep switching costs is correct, the -9.0% five-day decline is material and extended. This is not light-volume sympathy noise; the 1.24x volume multiple indicates institutional participation in the selloff. The bull case acknowledges this is 'broad SaaS de-rating rather than name-specific impairment,' yet still classifies it as OVERDONE. If the move reflects genuine sector re-pricing of SaaS multiples on rising rates or growth deceleration concerns (separate from AI hype), then MANH may have legitimate downside ahead, not just sympathy overshoot. (Haiku's original BUY reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 4, threat_assessment='minimal', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Enterprise WMS subscriptions with 9-12 month sales cycles and deep hardware integration are orthogonal to smart-home speaker features. Filings evidence emphasizes internal operational risk and regulated-industry adoption risk, not replacement threat. Move -5.24% on 1.24x volume, but five-day change -9.0% shows already-extended decline (SaaS de-rating more than name-specific). Part of Gemini basket — exploratory tier per basket rule.)

red_team.weakest_link: The five-day decline of -9.0% combined with the -5.24% single-day move suggests the selloff has already materialized and may reflect broader SaaS sector concerns beyond AI-event sympathy.
red_team.critique: While the bull thesis that enterprise WMS systems have deep switching costs is correct, the -9.0% five-day decline is material and extended. This is not light-volume sympathy noise; the 1.24x volume multiple indicates institutional participation in the selloff. The bull case acknowledges this is 'broad SaaS de-rating rather than name-specific impairment,' yet still classifies it as OVERDONE. If the move reflects genuine sector re-pricing of SaaS multiples on rising rates or growth deceleration concerns (separate from AI hype), then MANH may have legitimate downside ahead, not just sympathy overshoot.

entries.name: Paycom Software, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A consumer Gemini speaker has no overlap with payroll tax compliance, but PAYC's filings do reveal a real second-order AI risk (employee headcount compression at customers). Move is partial — five-day decline already extended. The Google Home trigger specifically is not the right driver; this is broader AI-on-HR-software anxiety.
entries.what_kills: Material guide-down on per-employee pricing or client headcount; a major HR-AI agent launch from Microsoft/Google specifically targeting SMB payroll; further breakdown of the already-extended 5-day chart.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3 (at threshold), threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. Filings do reveal real second-order AI risk (employee headcount compression at customer firms = less per-employee revenue). This is a genuine, if indirect, exposure. However, the move (-4.19%, five-day -9.51%) is already extended and the Google Home trigger is misaligned with the actual risk vector (HR/payroll AI agents, not consumer speakers). Confidence at threshold + threat is indirect + panic is partial (not unjustified) = WATCH. The threshold-confidence rule applies here.

entries.name: Paylocity Holding Corporation
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: Same logic as PAYC — Google Home doesn't file 941s or handle state UI registrations. However the move is on top of an already-extended 5-day decline, suggesting the broader HR/payroll AI fear is in motion. Sympathy fade is mis-targeted to this specific Gemini trigger.
entries.what_kills: An AI-native payroll competitor gaining material SMB traction; client headcount compression; further leg down on rising volume.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3 (at threshold), threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. Same as PAYC: real but indirect AI exposure (employee headcount risk), already-extended five-day move (-9.97%), Google Home trigger is misaligned with actual risk. Confidence at threshold + threat indirect + panic partial = WATCH. Need data on SMB churn before committing capital.

entries.name: Duolingo, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: The Google Home speaker trigger is unrelated to language learning, but DUOL has real (though manageable) exposure to consumer AI products. The dopamine-loop/streak moat is the defense, and DUOL is monetizing AI via Duolingo Max. Move on light volume looks more like consumer-AI basket fade than fundamental threat.
entries.what_kills: A flagship consumer AI tutor product (ChatGPT Tutor, Gemini Tutor) gaining material DAU traction; declining DAUs or streak metrics; mobile platform fee or policy changes from Apple/Google.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3, threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='partial'. DUOL has real (though manageable) indirect exposure: translation services may reduce demand for language learning, and consumer AI tutoring apps are a legitimate competitive threat. However, DUOL's own moat is gamification/streak loops and they are already monetizing GenAI via Duolingo Max. Move -3.65% on light volume, five-day +1.21% shows no downside momentum. Confidence at threshold + threat indirect + panic partial = WATCH.

entries.name: Photronics, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: A consumer OS release adding Gemini features to Android phones has zero mechanical connection to photomask demand. If anything, more AI compute = more advanced-node chip designs = more photomask demand. The selloff is pure thematic semi-sympathy tape with the sign flipped — Photronics is a beneficiary of AI capex, not a victim of a consumer mobile feature release.
entries.what_kills: An actual cyclical downturn in semi capex unrelated to today's trigger; loss of a top-two customer; further Section 232 / China export restrictions that materially reduce PLAB's addressable Asia revenue (82% of total).
entries.reasoning: Already a held position at 7 days old, +15.09% recovery. New flag is a duplicate (second flag on PLAB from Gemini/Android catalyst) confirming same thesis. Do not ADD or repurchase; Screen 1 does not support ADD positions due to fixed 5-15 day window discipline.

entries.name: Health Catalyst, Inc
entries.catalyst: Android 17 launches with new multitasking tools as Google expands Gemini features
entries.thesis: HCAT has plenty of real issues (DOS-to-Ignite migration churn, multiple restructurings, end-market funding cuts) but a consumer Android Gemini release is not one of them. HIPAA, BAA requirements, and EHR integration depth prevent general-purpose AI agents from touching provider data. The drop is sympathy-fade on top of an already-pressured name. Confidence intentionally limited because HCAT has genuine, name-specific issues that make this less clean.
entries.what_kills: Further Medicaid funding cuts pressuring health-system IT spend; client losses to Epic/Oracle accelerating; Ignite migration causing more revenue churn than guided.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 2 (below min_buy_confidence threshold of 3). Filings show pre-existing competitive risk (EHR players entering analytics), not specific AI-consumer-release threat. Move -3.26% on very light volume 0.43x, but name has genuine underlying issues (DOS-to-Ignite churn, restructuring, funding cuts) that make this less clean sympathy. Low confidence + genuine name-specific headwinds = skip.

entries.name: Omnicell, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
entries.thesis: A Gemini smart speaker cannot unlock a narcotic cabinet or dispense Schedule II controlled substances. OMCL's physical robotics moat in hospital pharmacies is essentially zero overlap with consumer AI. Move on average volume but no company news — pure thematic fade.
entries.what_kills: Hospital customer capital budget compression; a major customer cancellation; deterioration in SaaS retention.
entries.reasoning: Already a held position at 7 days old, -3.86% P&L. New flag is a duplicate (second flag on OMCL from Gemini/Android catalyst, confidence 4, threat='none') reinforcing same thesis. Do not ADD or repurchase; Screen 1 does not support ADD positions.

entries.name: Stride, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's new 'AI Mode' on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
entries.thesis: Stride's revenue is contractually tied to per-pupil state funding for accredited virtual public schools — parents enroll children for diplomas, transcripts, and state-funded education, none of which a Meta Facebook AI feature can deliver. The sympathy-fade premise ('AI tutors replace edtech') is the wrong frame; Stride is a regulated school operator, not a tutoring app. The move appears to be an extreme overreaction conflating Stride with consumer edtech apps.
entries.what_kills: Evidence of state legislatures cutting virtual charter funding, charter non-renewals, or material enrollment declines in the next data release; a concurrent negative catalyst we missed.
entries.reasoning: Already a held position at 7 days old, -14.39% P&L. New flag from Meta AI Mode catalyst (confidence 3, threat='minimal') reinforces thesis that Stride's regulated K-12 school-as-a-service model is orthogonal to consumer AI tutors. However, already making position adjustment (TRIM) above due to severity of move. Do not buy additional shares.

entries.name: nCino, Inc.
entries.catalyst: Meta's new 'AI Mode' on Facebook pulls from public info across its platforms
entries.thesis: nCino sells loan-origination workflow software to regulated banks where examination compliance, multi-year deployments, and integration with core banking systems form structural moats. A Meta consumer AI Mode for Facebook has zero plausible blast radius into bank loan-origination software. The negative move is pure thematic SaaS-AI sympathy.
entries.what_kills: Evidence that a major bank customer is replacing nCino with an in-house AI/LLM-based workflow; deterioration in subscription bookings; renewal rate decline.
entries.reasoning: Classification OVERDONE, confidence 3 (at threshold), threat_assessment='indirect', panic_calibration='unjustified'. Filings show nCino itself investing in AI as core growth strategy, but Meta consumer AI Mode has zero plausible threat to regulated bank loan-origination workflow software. Move -3.79% on light volume 0.60x, five-day -1.99%. Confidence at threshold + threat indirect = WATCH. Understand the competitive threat vector before committing capital.
